vector<string> generateSubstrings(const string& s) {
    vector<string> subs;
    int n = s.length();
    for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
        for (int j = i; j < n; ++j) {

}

But then, for the code in the Solution class:

int longestPalindrome(string s, string t) {
    vector<string> s_subs = generateSubstrings(s);
    vector<string> t_subs = generateSubstrings(t);
    int max_len = 0;
    for (const string& s_sub : s_subs) {
        for (const string& t_sub : t_subs) {
            string combined = s_sub + t_sub;
            if (isPalindrome(combined)) {
                if (combined.length() > max_len) {
                    max_len = combined.length();
                }
            }
        }
    }
    return max_len;
}

But then, the helper function isPalindrome:

bool isPalindrome(const string& s) {
    int left = 0, right = s.length() - 1;
    while (left < right) {
        if (s[left] != s[right]) {
            return false;
        }
        ++left;
        --right;
    }
    return true;
}

class Solution {
public:
    int longestPalindrome(string s, string t) {
        vector<string> s_subs = generateSubstrings(s);
        vector<string> t_subs = generateSubstrings(t);
        int max_len = 0;
        for (const string& s_sub : s_subs) {
            for (const string& t_sub : t_subs) {
                string combined = s_sub + t_sub;
                if (isPalindrome(combined)) {
                    max_len = max(max_len, (int)combined.length());
                }
            }
        }
        return max_len;
    }
private:
    vector<string> generateSubstrings(const string& s) {
        vector<string> subs;
        int n = s.length();
        for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
            for (int j = i; j < n; ++j) {
                subs.push_back(s.substr(i, j - i + 1));
            }
        }
        subs.push_back(""); // add empty substring
        return subs;
    }
    bool isPalindrome(const string& s) {
        int left = 0, right = s.length() - 1;
        while (left < right) {
            if (s[left] != s[right]) {
                return false;
            }
            ++left;
            --right;
        }
        return true;
    }
};

So the code should be as follows:

class Solution {
public:
    int longestPalindrome(string s, string t) {
        vector<string> s_subs = generateSubstrings(s);
        vector<string> t_subs = generateSubstrings(t);
        int max_len = 0;
        for (const string& s_sub : s_subs) {
            for (const string& t_sub : t_subs) {
                string combined = s_sub + t_sub;
                if (isPalindrome(combined)) {
                    max_len = max(max_len, (int)combined.length());
                }
            }
        }
        return max_len;
    }
private:
    vector<string> generateSubstrings(const string& s) {
        vector<string> subs;
        int n = s.length();
        for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
            for (int j = i; j < n; ++j) {
                subs.push_back(s.substr(i, j - i + 1));
            }
        }
        subs.push_back(""); // add empty substring
        return subs;
    }
    bool isPalindrome(const string& s) {
        int left = 0, right = s.length() - 1;
        while (left < right) {
            if (s[left] != s[right]) {
                return false;
            }
            ++left;
            --right;
        }
        return true;
    }
};

vector<string> generateSubstrings(const string& s) {
    vector<string> subs;
    int n = s.length();
    for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
        for (int j = i; j < n; ++j) {
            subs.push_back(s.substr(i, j - i + 1));
        }
    }
    subs.push_back(""); // add empty substring
    return subs;
}

But then, for the code in the Solution class:

int longestPalindrome(string s, string t) {
    vector<string> s_subs = generateSubstrings(s);
    vector<string> t_subs = generateSubstrings(t);
    int max_len = 0;
    for (const string& s_sub : s_subs) {
        for (const string& t_sub : t_subs) {
            string combined = s_sub + t_sub;
            if (isPalindrome(combined)) {
                if (combined.length() > max_len) {
                    max_len = combined.length();
                }
            }
        }
    }
    return max_len;
}

But then, the helper function isPalindrome:

bool isPalindrome(const string& s) {
    int left = 0, right = s.length() - 1;
    while (left < right) {
        if (s[left] != s[right]) {
            return false;
        }
        ++left;
        --right;
    }
    return true;
}

But in C++, the code for the Solution class would need to have the helper functions. However, since the helper functions are not part of the public API, perhaps they can be written as static functions inside the class.

But for the code to compile, perhaps the code can be written as follows:

class Solution {
public:
    int longestPalindrome(string s, string t) {
        vector<string> s_subs = generateSubstrings(s);
        vector<string> t_subs = generateSubstrings(t);
        int max_len = 0;
        for (const string& s_sub : s_subs) {
            for (const string& t_sub : t_subs) {
                string combined = s_sub + t_sub;
                if (isPalindrome(combined)) {
                    max_len = max(max_len, (int)combined.length());
                }
            }
        }
        return max_len;
    }
private:
    vector<string> generateSubstrings(const string& s) {
        vector<string> subs;
        int n = s.length();
        for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
            for (int j = i; j < n; ++j) {
                subs.push_back(s.substr(i, j - i + 1));
            }
        }
        subs.push_back(""); // add empty substring
        return subs;
    }
    bool isPalindrome(const string& s) {
        int left = 0, right = s.length() - 1;
        while (left < right) {
            if (s[left] != s[right]) {
                return false;
            }
            ++left;
            --right;
        }
        return true;
    }
};

But what about the case where the maximum is achieved by taking empty from s and a substring from t? The code would generate that as well.

Yes. Because the generateSubstrings function includes the empty string for t_subs. So when s_sub is empty and t_sub is a substring from t, the code checks the combined string (t_sub) for being a palindrome.

So this code should handle all cases.
